raw_output:
```
══════════════════════════════════════════════
  CODE REVIEW REPORT
  Files changed: 1 | Lines added: +85 | Lines removed: -0
══════════════════════════════════════════════

── BLOCKERS (4) ────────────────────────────

[B1] src/api/dashboard.ts:18-26 — N+1 query pattern will cause severe performance degradation
     Nested loops make individual database calls for each order item's product. With 10 orders
     containing 5 items each, this executes 50+ sequential database queries. At scale, this
     endpoint will time out or severely degrade.
     Fix: Collect all product IDs first, then fetch all products in a single query:
     ```typescript
     const productIds = orders.flatMap(o => o.items.map(i => i.productId));
     const products = await db.products.find({ _id: { $in: productIds } });
     const productMap = new Map(products.map(p => [p._id, p]));
     ```

[B2] src/api/dashboard.ts:21 — Missing null check will crash when product is deleted
     `db.products.findById(item.productId)` returns null/undefined if the product was deleted,
     but the code immediately accesses `product.name` without checking. This will throw a
     TypeError at runtime.
     Fix: Add null check and handle gracefully:
     ```typescript
     const product = await db.products.findById(item.productId);
     if (!product) continue; // or use default values
     ```

[B3] src/api/dashboard.ts:64 — Missing null check on deleted product
     Same issue as B2. `db.products.findById(view.productId)` can return null, but code
     accesses properties directly. Will crash when a viewed product has been deleted.
     Fix: Add null check before pushing to products array.

[B4] src/api/dashboard.ts:10,40,55,79 — Non-null assertions will crash if auth middleware fails
     Using `req.user!.id` assumes `req.user` exists, but there's no runtime guard. If authentication
     middleware is misconfigured or bypassed, this throws "Cannot read property 'id' of undefined".
     Fix: Add explicit guard at the start of each handler:
     ```typescript
     if (!req.user) {
       res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' });
       return;
     }
     const userId = req.user.id;
     ```

── WARNINGS (5) ────────────────────────────

[W1] src/api/dashboard.ts:42-43 — Inefficient aggregation using application-level reduce
     Fetches all completed orders then sums them in Node.js. This should be a database aggregation
     query to avoid loading potentially thousands of order documents into memory.
     Suggestion: Use `db.orders.aggregate([{ $match: {...} }, { $group: { _id: null, total: { $sum: '$total' }, count: { $sum: 1 } } }])`

[W2] src/api/dashboard.ts:63-69 — N+1 query for recently viewed products
     Fetches 10 products individually in a loop. Less severe than B1 due to the limit, but still
     inefficient. Should collect product IDs and fetch in a single query.
     Suggestion: Apply the same batching pattern as recommended in B1.

[W3] src/api/dashboard.ts:14 — Missing pagination will load unbounded data
     `db.orders.find({ userId, status: 'completed' })` loads ALL completed orders for a user.
     For active customers with hundreds or thousands of orders, this will cause memory issues
     and slow response times.
     Suggestion: Add pagination parameters: `limit` and `skip` from query params.

[W4] src/api/dashboard.ts:10-85 — No error handling for database failures
     None of the async database calls are wrapped in try-catch blocks. Any database connection
     issue, timeout, or query error will result in an unhandled promise rejection.
     Suggestion: Wrap handlers in try-catch and return appropriate error responses.

[W5] src/api/dashboard.ts:1-85 — No test coverage for new endpoints
     Four new endpoint handlers with complex database logic and multiple edge cases (deleted
     products, pagination, null values) have no corresponding tests.
     Suggestion: Add integration tests covering at least: successful responses, deleted product
     handling, empty result sets, and error cases.

── SUGGESTIONS (2) ──────────────────────────

[S1] src/api/dashboard.ts:3 — Unused import
     `OrderItem` type is imported but never used in this file.

[S2] src/api/dashboard.ts:60 — Magic number should be a constant
     `limit: 10` is hardcoded. Consider defining `const RECENTLY_VIEWED_LIMIT = 10` at the
     module level for easier configuration.

── VERDICT ─────────────────────────────────

  ✗ NOT READY — 4 blocker(s) must be resolved before merging.

     Critical issues: N+1 query pattern will cause production performance
     problems, missing null checks will cause crashes, and unsafe type
     assertions create reliability risks.

══════════════════════════════════════════════
```

── ORCHESTRATOR SUMMARY ────────────────────────
{"blockers": 4, "warnings": 5, "suggestions": 2, "verdict": "NOT_READY"}
────────────────────────────────────────────────

but the code immediately accesses `product.name` without checking. This will throw a

Fix: Add null check and handle gracefully:
```typescript
const product = await db.products.findById(item.productId);
if (!product) continue; // or use default values
```
